title: Workplace Access & Resource Request Management System — Full UI Build

requirements.text: Apply a professional enterprise visual theme across the entire application: deep navy/slate primary palette with teal/cyan accents, clean sans-serif typography (Inter or similar), card-based layouts with subtle shadows, status-color coding (yellow=Submitted, blue=Approved, green=Completed, red=Rejected, orange=In Fulfillment), and a collapsible left sidebar navigation. Ensure the design is responsive and mobile-friendly.

quotes: The interface shall be responsive and mobile-friendly.
Users shall be able to complete a request within 3 steps.

acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette and typography applied across all pages
Collapsible left sidebar with navigation links to all modules
Status badges use the defined color coding system
Layout is usable on mobile screen widths

requirements.text: Implement a Login Page with a company-branded sign-in form (email + password fields), an Internet Identity login button, and an MFA indicator badge. The page must show a role selector (Employee, Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin) to simulate RBAC personas for demo purposes.

quotes: The system shall implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

acceptanceCriteria: Login form renders with email, password fields and a sign-in button
Role selector allows switching between Employee, Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin
MFA badge/indicator is visible on the page
Successful login navigates to the Dashboard

requirements.text: Build a backend data model and actor in Motoko to store: Users (id, name, role, department, managerDelegate), Requests (id, employeeId, type, category, justification, status, riskLevel, priority, createdAt, slaDeadline, estimatedCompletion, approvalChain, currentApprover, cost), AuditLogs (id, requestId, actorId, action, timestamp, notes), and Notifications (id, userId, message, read, createdAt). Expose query and update functions for CRUD operations on all entities.
acceptanceCriteria: All four data types are defined as stable variables in main.mo
CRUD functions are callable for Requests, Users, AuditLogs, and Notifications
Request status can be updated through an updateRequestStatus function
Approval chain is stored as an array of approver role strings per request

requirements.text: Create a main Dashboard page showing: (1) a summary row of KPI cards — Total Requests, Pending Approvals, Completed This Month, SLA Breaches; (2) a recent requests table with columns for Request ID, Type, Status badge, Submitted Date, and SLA countdown; (3) a requests-by-status donut chart; (4) a requests-by-category bar chart. The dashboard must adapt its visible actions based on the logged-in role.
acceptanceCriteria: Four KPI cards display with correct labels and mock/live data
Recent requests table renders with status badges and SLA countdown timers
Donut chart shows request distribution by status
Bar chart shows request distribution by category
Role-specific action buttons differ between Employee and Admin views

requirements.text: Build a Create Request page with a multi-step wizard (3 steps max): Step 1 — select request type (System Access, Physical Access, Equipment, Software Licenses) with icon cards; Step 2 — fill request details form (sub-category, justification text, priority selector, cost estimate, document attachment field); Step 3 — AI Pre-Submission Analysis panel showing duplicate access warnings, missing info alerts, suggested access level, and recommended approval chain, with a 'Confirm & Submit' button. After submit, save the request to the backend.

quotes: AI analyzes the request before submission
Checks if the employee already has similar access

acceptanceCriteria: Three-step wizard with progress indicator renders correctly
Step 1 shows four request type cards with icons
Step 2 form captures all required fields including justification and priority
Step 3 displays AI analysis panel with simulated suggestions and warnings
Submitting the form creates a new request record in the backend
User is redirected to the request detail page after submission

requirements.text: Build a Request Detail Page showing: full request metadata (type, category, status, priority, cost, justification), an approval chain timeline (showing each approver node, their status, and timestamp), a status progress stepper (Submitted → Under Review → Approved → In Fulfillment → Completed), a SLA countdown timer, AI risk flags panel (e.g., 'Warning: user already has admin access in 3 systems'), and an activity/audit log feed at the bottom.
acceptanceCriteria: All request metadata fields are displayed
Approval chain renders as a vertical timeline with approver names and statuses
Status stepper highlights the current stage
SLA countdown timer counts down from the deadline
AI risk flags panel shows warning messages when applicable
Audit log lists all actions taken on the request with timestamps

requirements.text: Build an Approvals Screen (visible to Manager, IT Admin, Finance Admin roles) listing all requests pending their approval. Each row shows: requester name, request type, risk level badge, cost, and action buttons (Approve, Reject, Request More Info). Implement intelligent approval routing logic in the backend: low-risk software requests are auto-approved; high-risk requests require Manager + IT Security; requests over $1,000 require Finance approval. Approving or rejecting a request updates its status and appends an audit log entry.

quotes: High-risk server access → Manager + IT Security + Compliance

acceptanceCriteria: Approvals list page renders with pending requests filtered by logged-in approver role
Risk level badge is shown per request row
Approve and Reject buttons update request status in the backend
Auto-approval logic sets low-risk software requests to Approved without manual action
High-risk requests display multi-level approval chain
Audit log entry is created on each approval/rejection action

requirements.text: Build an AI Chatbot Assistant panel (floating button on all pages that expands to a sidebar chat). The chatbot accepts natural language input, simulates NLP parsing to extract structured fields (category, priority, quantity, reason), displays the parsed request summary, and offers a one-click 'Create This Request' button that pre-fills the Create Request form. Include example prompt suggestions like 'I need access to CRM for a marketing campaign' and 'Need laptop urgently for new joiner tomorrow'.
acceptanceCriteria: Floating chatbot button is visible on all pages
Chatbot sidebar opens on click and accepts text input
Entering a natural language request displays a structured parsed output (category, priority, quantity, reason)
A 'Create This Request' button pre-fills the Create Request wizard
Suggested example prompts are shown when the chat is empty

requirements.text: Build a Reports & Export page (Admin/Management role only) with: (1) a filter bar for date range, department, and request type; (2) a summary statistics section with charts (requests over time line chart, approval rate pie chart, SLA compliance bar chart); (3) export buttons for CSV download and a printable PDF summary view; (4) a department-wise breakdown table; (5) an audit trail log table with search and pagination. Generate report data from the backend's stored requests and audit logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Reports page is accessible only to Admin/Management roles
Filter bar updates all charts and tables on apply
Line chart shows request volume over time
CSV export downloads a correctly formatted file with request data
PDF print view renders a clean summary layout
Department breakdown table groups requests by department with counts and SLA compliance %
Audit trail table supports text search and pagination

requirements.text: Build an Admin Configuration page with tabs for: (1) User Management — list users, assign roles, set manager delegates; (2) SLA Rules — configure SLA timelines per request category; (3) Approval Chain Policies — define which roles approve which request types and risk levels; (4) System Notifications — toggle in-app notification preferences. All configuration changes must persist to the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Config page renders with four tabs
User Management tab lists all users and allows role and delegate assignment
SLA Rules tab allows editing deadline hours per request category
Approval Chain Policies tab shows and allows editing of routing rules per risk level
All changes are persisted to backend stable storage

requirements.text: Implement an in-app notification system: a bell icon in the top navigation bar shows unread notification count badge. Clicking opens a notification dropdown listing alerts such as 'Your request #123 has been approved', 'SLA breach warning for request #456', and 'New request pending your approval'. Notifications are fetched from the backend and can be marked as read.

quotes: The system shall notify users and approvers via email or in-app alerts.

acceptanceCriteria: Bell icon in navbar shows unread count badge
Clicking bell opens a dropdown with notification list
Each notification shows message text and timestamp
Mark as read updates the notification in the backend and removes the badge count

constraints: Do not integrate external AI services (OpenAI, LangChain) — simulate AI analysis, NLP parsing, and chatbot responses with rule-based frontend logic and mock data
Do not use PostgreSQL, FastAPI, or Node.js — use Motoko single-actor backend on Internet Computer
Do not implement actual email sending — use in-app notifications only
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui/
All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: AI Code Quality Checker microservice (LangChain/LlamaIndex/CrewAI agent — not supported)
Multi-Factor Authentication via external MFA provider

required: Enterprise application logo mark — shield icon with a checkmark and flowing lines suggesting access control, on a transparent background (accessflow-logo.dim_128x128.png)
Abstract hero background for the login page — deep navy blue geometric mesh pattern with subtle teal gradient glow nodes (login-bg.dim_1440x900.png)
